channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Dire Wolf Launches Kickstarter for War of the Dragon: The Wheel of Time
item.content:encoded:
The Wheel of Time series is getting its first official board game adaptation. Tabletop publisher Dire Wolf's adaptation, War of the Dragon: The Wheel of Time will be bringing the iconic fantasy setting has gone live on Kickstarter, and it is blowing up like Balefire.

According to its campaign page, War of the Dragon: The Wheel of Time is a grand strategy board game. Players will need to set into the roles of the grand forces at the heart of The Wheel of Time: Rand al'Thor, The Eternal Dragon and his eternal nemesis, The Dark One. In these roles, you will wage war across a land of magic, prophecies, and powerful armies.

The gameplay will feature a blend of tableau building, action selection, and area control mechanics. Players will take turns playing cards that will generate resources, recruit allies, or allow forces to move across the map. Certain actions will even progress your Wheel, which tracks the progress of your character's destiny. 

In addition, the game will feature two modes of play. Hero Mode, recommended for newbies, has two-to-four players gather trusted allies and lieutenants to complete missions gain control of different regions. For more experienced players, there is Epic Mode. This has two players control massive armies, upgrading them and enhancing their abilities throughout the events of the game.

For fans of the novels, War of the Dragon: The Wheel of Time adapts the years leading up to The Battle of Two Rivers as depicted in The Shadow Rising to the end of the Third Age. Epic Mode specifically is mean to adapt the cataclysmic conclusion of The Last Battle. Who will remain standing? That is a matter for your table to decide.

The studio behind the project, Dire Wolf has a respectable place in the tabletop industry. Their body of work has included Dune: Imperium, a big box adaptation of Frank Herbert's epic sci-fi series, the critically acclaimed Clank! series, and even a deck-building game based on the superhero series Invincible, 

War of the Dragon: The Wheel of Time will be available in two editions across three pledge tiers. The Dragon Reborn Edition will come to retail stores in 2027, containing all gameplay elements and cardboard standees. This can be backed at $90. 

The second version, The One Power Edition, will be a campaign exclusive, and feature forty highly detailed miniatures of iconic characters from the series. This edition can be backed at an MSRP of $123. If you want more, The Last Battle Bundle (MSRP of $289) will get you The One Power Edition, card sleeves, a neoprene playmat, and a Map of the Westlands art print.

At the time of writing, War of the Dragon: The Wheel of Time has raised $502,338, more than ten times its initial funding goal of $50,000.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/dire-wolf-launches-kickstarter-for-war-of-dragon-wheel-of-time
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sirius Dice Announces Official Critical Role Vox Machina Dice Set
item.content:encoded:
Critical Role fans have some new sets of dice to collect. Sirius Dice has announced a new line of Critical Role Vox Machina dice, bringing the personality of the actual play series' heroes to every gaming table's most important tool.

According to a press release from Sirius Dice, their Critical Role Vox Machina Dice sets will be complete sets of eight polyhedral dice. A d20, a d12, a d8, 2d10, a d6, and a d4, meaning a single set of dice will have you ready for any adventure you undertake, be it Dungeons & Dragons or Pathfinder. 

There will be seven sets of dice in total, with each one, both the packaging and the dice containing visual inspirations for each member of the adventuring party Vox Machina: Grog Strongjaw, Vex'alia, Vax'ildan, Pike Trickfoot, Scanlan Shorthalt, and Percival de Rolo.

The party of Vox Machina are the most iconic characters of Critical Role. The heroes of Campaign 1 of the actual play series captured the hearts of fans thanks to strong, committed performances by their players, and compelling, ongoing narratives brought on by dice rolls and choice. 

Their adventures have since been adapted into the Prime Video animated series, The Legend of Vox Machina, with its fourth season debuting June 3rd on the platform.

The press materials showcasing the Vox Machina dice continue Sirius' attention to detail. For example, Percival's dice appear metallic with flecks of gold and metal, blending his engineering skill with his royal background. In addition, the highest value on certain dice feature different bits of character iconography: his glasses, a bullet, and the Vox Machina symbol.

This care and craft is apparent in every set. Grog's dice being an earthy blend of red and black sporting tankards, his axe, and the Titanstone Knuckles. Vex and Vax's dice being translucent with different shades of blue denoting them as twins. Scanlan's dice having glitter, representing his love of theatrics and bardic performance. Pike's dice having a bit of a glow since she's a Cleric.

Thankfully, these Critical Role Vox Machina dice won't break the bank either. Each set will be available at an MSRP of $19.99 on Sirius' web store or at certain hobby stores near you. The dice sets will be available on July 2026.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler
